expectations.text: No file was created in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ (the production path)
expectations.evidence: Glob search of /Users/hariharansampathkumar/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ found no files dated 2026-03-19. All pre-existing files are dated 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-18. The executor explicitly wrote to the sandbox path: /Users/hariharansampathkumar/Documents/projects/ai_engineering/skills_engineering/learning-insights-eval-setup/iteration-2/sandbox/learning-insights/ai_engineering/2026-03-19_bm25-vs-dense-hybrid-retrieval.md — confirmed by the Write tool result in the transcript.

expectations.text: The response either asks for clarification OR captures the insight from the unambiguous single-topic conversation — either behavior is acceptable. Flag which one occurred.
expectations.evidence: The executor captured the insight without asking for clarification. The conversation was a 4-turn single-topic exchange about BM25 vs dense retrieval and hybrid systems. The executor's reasoning (transcript line 13): 'The intent is absent but the conversation is focused on a single topic throughout. I'll capture what's clearly there.' The response.txt confirms this: 'No intent was provided with the command, but the conversation covered a single focused topic throughout — BM25 vs dense retrieval and hybrid systems — so I captured that directly.' Behavior that occurred: direct capture (no clarification asked).

expectations.text: If a file was created, it is inside the sandbox path only
expectations.evidence: The insight file was written to /Users/hariharansampathkumar/Documents/projects/ai_engineering/skills_engineering/learning-insights-eval-setup/iteration-2/sandbox/learning-insights/ai_engineering/2026-03-19_bm25-vs-dense-hybrid-retrieval.md, confirmed by the Write tool result in the transcript. The INDEX.md was also written to the sandbox: sandbox/learning-insights/INDEX.md. No writes to the production path occurred.

claims.claim: The skill spec says to ask for clarification only if the intent is ambiguous — not just if intent is missing
claims.evidence: capture-insight.md line 104: 'If the intent is ambiguous or no clear insight matches it, ask the user to clarify.' The invocation was '/capture-insight' with no argument. The executor's interpretation that a single-topic conversation removes ambiguity is reasonable, though debatable — 'no intent' could also be read as triggering the clarification rule.

claims.claim: The insight file content accurately reflects the conversation
claims.evidence: The captured file's Aha Moment and One-Liner faithfully reflect the conversation: BM25 as keyword-based (TF-IDF), dense retrieval as embedding-based semantic search, hybrid via RRF. All technical details were present in the conversation turns — nothing was fabricated or embellished.

claims.claim: The conversation had no emotional reactions to preserve
claims.evidence: The conversation transcript shows neutral, technical exchanges ('Quick question', 'Makes sense', 'Exactly'). The executor did not fabricate emotional reactions, which is correct per the skill spec.

claims.claim: Sandbox override was respected for all file writes
claims.evidence: Three Write calls confirmed in transcript: (1) insight file to sandbox/ai_engineering/, (2) INDEX.md to sandbox/, (3) response.txt and run_summary.txt to eval outputs dir. No writes attempted to ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/.

suggestions.assertion: The response either asks for clarification OR captures the insight from the unambiguous single-topic conversation — either behavior is acceptable. Flag which one occurred.
suggestions.reason: This assertion accepts two opposite behaviors as equally valid, which weakens its discriminating power. A skill that always captures (never asks) and a skill that always asks (never captures) would both pass. Consider splitting into two separate test cases — one where the conversation is genuinely ambiguous (multiple unrelated topics) to test the clarification path, and one like this test where the topic is clear. The current formulation cannot distinguish correct behavior from a broken skill that always guesses right by coincidence.

No assertion checks the content quality of the captured insight — specifically whether the Struggle, Drill-Down, Aha Moment, and One-Liner sections are faithful to the conversation and not fabricated. A skill that writes a generic or hallucinated insight file would pass all three current assertions. Consider adding: 'The captured file's Aha Moment references BM25, dense retrieval, and RRF' or similar content-grounding check.

The 'no file in production path' assertion passes even if the executor simply failed to create any file at all. It conflates two distinct failure modes: (a) wrote to wrong location, and (b) wrote nothing. Consider pairing it with an assertion that a file WAS created somewhere (e.g., 'A file named 2026-03-19_*.md exists in the sandbox path').

eval_feedback.overall: All three assertions pass on genuine correct behavior, but each has a gap. The behavior assertion is under-discriminating (accepts both outcomes), the sandbox assertion doesn't check file existence, and no assertion verifies content correctness. The eval would benefit from 2-3 targeted content assertions.
